howItWorks.intro: This haunted location risk calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Risk Score
rules.formula: Score = Age Factor + Deaths×10 + Reports×5 + Cemetery Bonus
rules.explanation: Weighted sum of historical and environmental factors.

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What makes a location haunted?
faq.answer: Allegedly: violent deaths, old buildings, proximity to burial sites, and repeated reports.

faq.question: Do old buildings have more activity?
faq.answer: Older buildings have more reported sightings, though this may correlate with more history.

faq.question: Is the Haunted Location Risk Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What building age should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate building age value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Haunted Location Risk Calculator?
sections.body: The Haunted Location Risk Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your building age, known deaths on property, previous reports, you get instant results including activity probability, risk category. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting activity probability right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your building age and need to find the right activity probability. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Building Age (years)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your building age — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: known deaths on property, previous reports, near cemetery/battlefield. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your activity probability, risk category. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your building age before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both activity probability and risk category — they work together to give you the full picture.
